app.name: Stratoclave
app.tagline: A unified proxy gateway for Amazon Bedrock.

login.title: Stratoclave
login.card_description: Sign in with the email and password issued by your administrator.
login.cli_footer: After signing in with the CLI, run <1>{{cmd}}</1> to bring this authenticated tab back into focus.
login.tagline_strato: STRATO · the stratum of governance layered on top of Bedrock
login.tagline_conclave: CONCLAVE · a sealed space where a tenant holds its credits in confidence

dashboard.label: Dashboard
dashboard.intro: See your credit balance, tenant and role at a glance. Use the top navigation to dig into each section.
dashboard.stat_role_footer: Roles are sourced from the DynamoDB Users table as the single source of truth.
dashboard.shortcut_my_usage_desc: Inspect token consumption by model and day.
dashboard.shortcut_api_keys_desc: Mint and manage long-lived API keys for external gateway clients such as cowork.
dashboard.shortcut_admin_users_desc: Create users, assign tenants, and adjust credit.
dashboard.shortcut_admin_tenants_desc: List all tenants, reassign owners and inspect their usage.
dashboard.shortcut_trusted_accounts_desc: Manage per-AWS-account SSO acceptance policies and pre-issued invites.
dashboard.shortcut_team_lead_desc: Create and inspect tenants you own: members, usage and settings.
dashboard.me_error: Failed to load your profile. Please wait a moment and reload.

admin_user_new.title: New user
admin_user_new.intro: A temporary password is issued via Cognito AdminCreateUser. The user updates it on first sign-in.
admin_user_new.basic_desc: Email is used as the Cognito username and is checked for duplicates.
admin_user_new.role_user_desc: May call the Messages API and view their own credit only.
admin_user_new.role_team_lead_desc: Owns their tenants + sees member usage.
admin_user_new.role_admin_desc: Full access to tenants, users and usage.
admin_user_new.tenant_card_desc: Default falls back to default-org, with the tenant default_credit as starting balance.
admin_user_new.tenant_help: If no tenant is picked the user joins default-org and receives the global 100,000 token allowance.
admin_user_new.credit_placeholder: Leave blank to use the tenant default_credit
admin_user_new.locale_help: Users can change this from the header after they sign in.
admin_user_new.error_email: Email is empty or does not contain an '@'.

admin_tenant_detail.members_desc: Only active UserTenants rows are shown. Archived history remains in UsageLogs.
admin_tenant_detail.members_empty: This tenant has no active members.
admin_tenant_detail.usage_by_model_empty: No usage data for this tenant yet.
admin_tenant_detail.owner_desc: Pick a user with the team_lead role, or set the owner to admin-owned (shared).
admin_tenant_detail.archive_desc: Sets status=archived while records and usage history are preserved. A tenant with the same name can be created immediately afterwards without collision.

access_denied.title: You do not have access to this page
access_denied.description: You may be missing the required role or you are not the owner of this tenant.

temp_password.title: Hand the temporary password to the user securely
temp_password.desc: This dialog will not be shown again. After handing it over, ask the user to set a new password right away.
temp_password.policy_note: The user must set a new password on first sign-in (Cognito NEW_PASSWORD_REQUIRED challenge).
temp_password.acknowledge: I have copied and will close

admin_trusted_account_detail.role_patterns_desc: Empty means every role is allowed.
admin_trusted_account_detail.invites_desc_base: Invites always take precedence. On top of auto_provision, they are the way to map Isengard / IAM users / per-role escalations to a specific email when the session_name is not itself an email.
admin_trusted_account_detail.invites_current_invite_only: (currently invite_only: users without an invite are rejected.)
admin_trusted_account_detail.invites_current_auto_provision: (currently auto_provision: users without an invite have their email derived from session_name.)
admin_trusted_account_detail.invites_empty: No invites for this account yet.
admin_trusted_account_detail.edit_desc: account_id cannot be changed. Other parameters can be tuned.
admin_trusted_account_detail.delete_desc: Cognito entries for users provisioned via this account remain. Future SSO logins from this account will be rejected.
admin_trusted_account_detail.invite_dialog_desc: On first SSO login from account <1>{{id}}</1>, this email is auto-provisioned as a Stratoclave user.
admin_trusted_account_detail.invite_iam_user_label: IAM user name (optional, IAM user invites only)
admin_trusted_account_detail.invite_iam_user_help: IAM users cannot be mapped to an email via their ARN, so we map by this name.

admin_trusted_accounts.title: Trusted AWS accounts
admin_trusted_accounts.intro: Allowlist of AWS accounts whose SSO / STS logins are accepted. Each account controls its own provisioning policy (invite_only / auto_provision) and which identity types are allowed (IAM user, Instance Profile).
admin_trusted_accounts.instance_profile_desc: EC2 instance profiles are shared across every user on the instance and cannot be attributed to a single person. Keep this option off unless the instance is dedicated (for example, a personal CI runner).
admin_trusted_accounts.row_empty_line2: Click "Add account" to allowlist an AWS account.
admin_trusted_accounts.create_desc: Accept SSO logins from this account's IAM Identity Center / SAML / IAM users.
admin_trusted_accounts.policy_invite_only_desc: Users without an invite are rejected (strict). IAM users / Isengard and similar can still be mapped explicitly via invites.
admin_trusted_accounts.policy_auto_provision_desc: Automatically provision session_name as email even without an invite. Invites can still coexist.
admin_trusted_accounts.roles_label: Allowed role patterns (glob; empty = allow every role)
admin_trusted_accounts.allow_iam_user_desc: Long-lived access keys. When combined with invite_only, admins must pre-register each user.
admin_trusted_accounts.allow_ip_desc: Shared EC2 instance profiles cannot identify a person. Enable only on dedicated instances.

admin_usage_logs.title: All usage
admin_usage_logs.intro: Browse the UsageLogs table with filters. Queries are backed by a PK Query when tenant_id is set, a GSI Query when user_id is set, and a Scan otherwise (truncated at 100 rows).

admin_tenants.title: Tenant administration
admin_tenants.intro: Lists every tenant, including tenants owned by Team Leads. Editing is possible from the detail page.
admin_tenants.hint_desc: `admin-owned` marks a tenant shared across the whole organization. Tenants owned by a Team Lead show that Team Lead's user_id.
admin_tenants.hint_detail: To change the owner, use "Reassign owner" on the tenant detail page (useful for rescuing tenants orphaned by Cognito user deletion).
admin_tenants.create_desc: Setting the owner to `admin-owned` creates a tenant shared by the whole organization.

admin_user_detail.action_assign_desc: Move the user to a different tenant. Credit is reset and the user is forced to sign in again.
admin_user_detail.action_credit_desc: Overwrite the user's credit on their current tenant (user_override).
admin_user_detail.action_delete_desc: Removes the user from Cognito and archives their UserTenants rows (UsageLogs is retained).
admin_user_detail.sso_card_desc: This user was provisioned via AWS SSO / STS.
admin_user_detail.assign_step1_desc: Select the target tenant, role and credit override.
admin_user_detail.assign_step2_desc: {{email}} will move from {{from}} to {{to}}. Enter the email to confirm.
admin_user_detail.assign_credit_placeholder: Defaults to the new tenant's default_credit
admin_user_detail.assign_task_archive: Archive the UserTenants row on the old tenant ({{from}}).
admin_user_detail.assign_task_activate: Activate the user on the new tenant ({{to}}) as new_role={{role}}.
admin_user_detail.assign_task_signout: Invalidate every session of the user (AdminUserGlobalSignOut).
admin_user_detail.credit_desc: Overwrite total_credit for {{email}} with a new value.
admin_user_detail.delete_desc: Removes the user from Cognito; UserTenants rows become archived. UsageLogs is retained for audit.

admin_users.title: User administration
admin_users.intro: Cross-cut management of the Cognito User Pool and the DynamoDB Users table.

team_lead_tenants.title: My tenants
team_lead_tenants.intro: Only the tenants you own are shown. Other tenants are hidden from both the list and direct URLs.
team_lead_tenants.empty_title: You have not created any tenants yet
team_lead_tenants.empty_desc: Click "New tenant" to create the first tenant you own.
team_lead_tenants.hint_title: What a Team Lead can do
team_lead_tenants.hint_desc: Create tenants you own, rename them, adjust default_credit, and view their members and usage. Tenant-user assignment requires an Administrator.

team_lead_tenant_new.title: New tenant
team_lead_tenant_new.intro: You are recorded as the owner. Ask an Administrator to bind users to this tenant.
team_lead_tenant_new.basic_desc: default_credit is used as the starting balance for new users attached to this tenant.
team_lead_tenant_new.error_limit: You have reached the maximum number of tenants a Team Lead can own (50). Archive an unused tenant or ask an Administrator.

me_usage.label: Usage history
me_usage.title: My usage history
me_usage.intro: Aggregated per-model consumption and the most recent requests, newest first.
me_usage.stat_tenants_footer: Includes tenants from past tenant switches.
me_usage.by_model_desc: Tokens consumed in this window, grouped by Bedrock model.
me_usage.empty_models: No Messages activity in this window.
me_usage.empty_history: No request history in this window.

me_api_keys.label: API Keys
me_api_keys.title: Long-lived API keys
me_api_keys.intro_lead: Mint long-lived API keys of the form <1>sk-stratoclave-…</1>. Clients such as Claude Desktop Cowork or your own gateway clients can then use Stratoclave as a Bedrock proxy. The plaintext is displayed only once at creation — store it safely.
me_api_keys.stat_storage_desc: The plaintext is never stored on the server.
me_api_keys.stat_blast_desc: Everything else is scope-gated. Immediate revocation is supported.
me_api_keys.list_desc: Revoke a key if a client has already cached its plaintext and needs to be cut off immediately.
me_api_keys.empty_message: You have not minted any API keys yet.
me_api_keys.revoke_desc: Revoking <1>{{id}}</1> will cause every client using this key to receive 401 immediately.
me_api_keys.revoke_ui_unsupported: Revoking from the UI is not yet supported. Please run the following from the CLI:
me_api_keys.revoke_hash_hint: The key_hash is <1>sha256(plaintext)</1>, computed locally where the plaintext is stored. Keys minted via the CLI print a <1>stratoclave api-key revoke …</1> hint at the end of the output.
me_api_keys.revoke_unsupported_error: This UI only supports revocation via key_hash. Please use the CLI (`stratoclave api-key revoke <hash>`).
me_api_keys.create_desc: Choose a label, scopes, and a lifetime. You can only grant a subset of your own role's permissions (roles: {{roles}}).
me_api_keys.create_expires_help: Shorter is safer; unlimited is supported if you need it.
me_api_keys.create_warning: After creation the plaintext is shown <1>once</1>. Closing the dialog makes it unrecoverable.
me_api_keys.created_desc: The plaintext is shown <1>only now</1>. Copy and store it before closing this dialog.
me_api_keys.created_hash_hint: To revoke later run: <1>stratoclave api-key revoke <hash></1>
me_api_keys.created_close: Close (the plaintext will no longer be visible)

team_lead_tenant_detail.members_card_desc: Your view only shows email and credit — user_id is intentionally hidden.
team_lead_tenant_detail.members_empty: No active members for this tenant yet. Ask an Administrator to bind users.
team_lead_tenant_detail.usage_card_desc: Aggregated by model and by member email. user_id is hidden in the Team Lead view.
team_lead_tenant_detail.by_model_empty: No usage data for this period yet.
team_lead_tenant_detail.access_denied_desc: Either you do not own it, or the tenant_id does not exist. Pick a tenant from "My tenants".
